key_insight: Foundational r$^2$SCAN-trained ML interatomic potentials can correct DFT formation energies toward experimental accuracy, and latent-feature delta-learning pushes errors below 50 meV/atom without extra DFT calculations.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical, reproducible path to rank crystal stability more reliably in materials screening pipelines using existing structures and low-cost post-processing corrections.
method: Compare MC3D thermodynamic stability against OQMD, Materials Project, and experimental formation enthalpies
Use PET-OMATPES foundational MLIP to replace/augment GGA-level formation energies
Train classical delta-learning models on latent MLIP features with regularization to refine energy corrections
evidence_signal: The abstract reports more than a 40% MAE reduction versus GGA and final errors below 50 meV/atom, comparable to experimental uncertainty.
watch_item: Corrections may alter relative phase stability if latent-feature models are not carefully regularized, so stability rankings should be checked for unintended shifts.
paper_title: Correcting DFT formation energies towards experimental accuracy using foundational MLIPs and latent-feature delta-learning
